en.extract:
The grey-throated warbler (Myiothlypis cinereicollis) is a species of bird in the family Parulidae.
It is found in Colombia and Venezuela. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist montane forests and heavily degraded former forest. There are three subspecies;

Myiothlypis cinereicollis cinereicollis (found in central Colombia to W Venezuela)
Myiothlypis cinereicollis pallidula (extreme NW of Sierra de Perijá. and N Colombia)
Myiothlypis cinereicollis zuliensis (Sierra de Perijá, Colombia, and NW Venezuela)

The '''grey-throated warbler''' ('''''Myiothlypis cinereicollis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Parulidae]].

It is found in [[Colombia]] and [[Venezuela]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s and heavily degraded former forest. There are three subspecies;

* ''Myiothlypis cinereicollis cinereicollis'' (found in central Colombia to W Venezuela)
* ''Myiothlypis cinereicollis pallidula'' (extreme NW of Sierra de Perijá. and N Colombia)
* ''Myiothlypis cinereicollis zuliensis'' (Sierra de Perijá, Colombia, and NW Venezuela)<ref>{{Cite web|last=Oiseaux.net|title=Paruline à cou gris - Myiothlypis cinereicollis - Grey-throated Warbler|url=https://www.oiseaux.net/birds/grey-throated.warbler.html|access-date=2020-06-20|website=www.oiseaux.net|language=en}}</ref>
